headline: Target & CrowdStrike earnings, Fed commentary: What to Watch
summary: Market Domination Overtime host Josh Lipton takes a look at the top stories for investors to watch on Tuesday, Mar. 2. More earnings are on deck, with retail names like Target (TGT), Best Buy (BBY), and Ross (ROST) reporting. On Holding (ONON) and CrowdStrike (CRWD) will also report results. Markets will hear commentary from Federal Reserve officials, including New York Fed President John Williams, Kansas City Fed President Jeff Schmid, and Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari. We will also be keeping an eye on further Iran war developments and oil's (CL=F, BZ=F) price action. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime.

headline: CrowdStrike Q4 earnings, gold prices fall on stronger US dollar
summary: Yahoo Finance Senior Producer John Hyland tracks Tuesday's top moving stocks and biggest market stories in this Market Minute. Gold (GC=F) prices are surprisingly lower despite extreme Iran-fueled market volatility. The price action comes as investors consider whether the US dollar (DX-Y.NYB) could become stronger. CrowdStrike (CRWD) is set to report fourth quarter earnings after markets close as investors continue to back away from the software (IGV) sector. Stay up to date on the latest market action, minute-by-minute, with Yahoo Finance's Market Minute.

content.title: CrowdStrike Q4 earnings, gold prices fall on stronger US dollar
content.description:
Yahoo Finance Senior Producer John Hyland tracks Tuesday's top moving stocks and biggest market stories in this Market Minute.

Gold (GC=F) prices are surprisingly lower despite extreme Iran-fueled market volatility. The price action comes as investors consider whether the US dollar (DX-Y.NYB) could become stronger.

CrowdStrike (CRWD) is set to report fourth quarter earnings after markets close as investors continue to back away from the software (IGV) sector.

Stay up to date on the latest market action, minute-by-minute, with Yahoo Finance's Market Minute.

headline: CrowdStrike: Fiscal Q4 Earnings Snapshot
summary: The average estimate of 15 analysts surveyed by Zacks Investment Research was for earnings of $1.10 per share. The cloud-based security company posted revenue of $1.31 billion in the period, which also topped Street forecasts. Fourteen analysts surveyed by Zacks expected $1.3 billion.

content.title: CrowdStrike: Fiscal Q4 Earnings Snapshot
content.summary: The average estimate of 15 analysts surveyed by Zacks Investment Research was for earnings of $1.10 per share. The cloud-based security company posted revenue of $1.31 billion in the period, which also topped Street forecasts. Fourteen analysts surveyed by Zacks expected $1.3 billion.

headline: CrowdStrike Earnings Beat Estimates. The Stock Is Flat.
summary: CrowdStrike Holdings  reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter results after the stock market closed on Tuesday.  CrowdStrike  posted fourth-quarter adjusted earnings of $1.12 a share, which came in above analyst estimates of $1.10 a share, according to FactSet.  Revenue for the quarter of $1.31 billion also beat Wall Street expectations of $1.3 billion.

content.title: CrowdStrike Earnings Beat Estimates. The Stock Is Flat.
content.summary: CrowdStrike Holdings  reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter results after the stock market closed on Tuesday.  CrowdStrike  posted fourth-quarter adjusted earnings of $1.12 a share, which came in above analyst estimates of $1.10 a share, according to FactSet.  Revenue for the quarter of $1.31 billion also beat Wall Street expectations of $1.3 billion.

headline: CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) Beats Q4 CY2025 Sales Expectations
summary: Cybersecurity platform provider CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) reported Q4 CY2025 results topping the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 23.3% year on year to $1.31 billion. Guidance for next quarter’s revenue was better than expected at $1.36 billion at the midpoint, 0.6% above analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $1.12 per share was 1.6% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) Beats Q4 CY2025 Sales Expectations
content.summary: Cybersecurity platform provider CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) reported Q4 CY2025 results topping the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 23.3% year on year to $1.31 billion. Guidance for next quarter’s revenue was better than expected at $1.36 billion at the midpoint, 0.6% above analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $1.12 per share was 1.6% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: CrowdStrike forecasts fiscal 2027 revenue above estimates on cybersecurity tools demand
summary: CrowdStrike forecast fiscal 2027 revenue above Wall Street estimates on Tuesday, betting on resilient demand for ‌its artificial intelligence-powered cybersecurity solutions. Shares of cybersecurity companies, including CrowdStrike, ‌have fallen recently as investors assessed the potential impact of AI startup Anthropic's Claude Code ​Security tool, even as some analysts said the worries were overblown. CrowdStrike's shares closed 1.7% higher.

content.title: CrowdStrike forecasts fiscal 2027 revenue above estimates on cybersecurity tools demand
content.summary: CrowdStrike forecast fiscal 2027 revenue above Wall Street estimates on Tuesday, betting on resilient demand for ‌its artificial intelligence-powered cybersecurity solutions. Shares of cybersecurity companies, including CrowdStrike, ‌have fallen recently as investors assessed the potential impact of AI startup Anthropic's Claude Code ​Security tool, even as some analysts said the worries were overblown. CrowdStrike's shares closed 1.7% higher.

headline: CrowdStrike Earnings Beat Estimates. The Stock Is Down.
summary: CrowdStrike Holdings  reported better-than-expected earnings after the stock market closed Tuesday.  CrowdStrike  posted fourth-quarter adjusted earnings of $1.12 a share, which came in above analyst estimates of $1.10 a share, according to FactSet.  Annual recurring revenue, or the annualized value of CrowdStrike’s customer subscription contracts, was $5.25 billion, a 24% increase from the prior year.

content.title: CrowdStrike Earnings Beat Estimates. The Stock Is Down.
content.summary: CrowdStrike Holdings  reported better-than-expected earnings after the stock market closed Tuesday.  CrowdStrike  posted fourth-quarter adjusted earnings of $1.12 a share, which came in above analyst estimates of $1.10 a share, according to FactSet.  Annual recurring revenue, or the annualized value of CrowdStrike’s customer subscription contracts, was $5.25 billion, a 24% increase from the prior year.

headline: CrowdStrike Swings to Profit, Sales Rise 23%

content.summary: The cybersecurity company posted a profit of $38.7 million, or 15 cents a share, in the quarter ended Jan. 31, compared with a loss of $86.3 million, or 35 cents a share, a year earlier.

headline: CrowdStrike (CRWD) Q4 2026 Earnings Transcript
summary: With me on the call are George Kurtz, Chief Executive Officer and Founder of CrowdStrike Holdings, Inc., and Burt Podbere, Chief Financial Officer.  For the year, we delivered $1.01 billion in net new ARR, up 25% year over year, our first year delivering over $1 billion of net new ARR.
